page: Microsoft Azure D8s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv3 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 8 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is limited to a 66 GB temporary disk, and the instance supports Premium Storage for external disk attachments.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the D8s_v3 sits primarily in the lower-tier performance brackets for modern workloads. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench is categorized as weak to poor, reflecting the older generation of the Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor. Multi-core performance is slightly better, reaching average levels in stress-ng multi-core tests, though Geekbench multi-core and software compilation benchmarks remain weak. Memory bandwidth tests show weak performance for both small and large block reads and writes, although PassMark memory scores achieve an average rating. LLM inference speeds for a small 135M parameter model show average performance, with prompt processing at 411.44 tokens per second and text generation at 157.1 tokens per second.

Given its hardware profile, the D8s_v3 offers a mature, stable environment under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, but it presents clear performance tradeoffs. It lacks modern instruction sets like AVX-512 or AMX, limiting its utility for advanced cryptography or dense matrix math. From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents a legacy hardware class that provides a predictable, dedicated resource allocation without the burstable performance drops of budget tiers, making it a stable choice for workloads that do not require high-frequency compute. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as light web serving, basic database operations, and small-scale application hosting where consistent resource availability is prioritized over raw processing speed.
description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores with hyper-threading) clocked at 2.3 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM and 66 GB of local storage. Benchmarks show weak single-core and multi-core CPU performance, alongside weak memory bandwidth, though it achieves average marks in multi-core stress-ng tests and small-model LLM inference. Lacking modern instruction sets like AVX-512, the D8s_v3 represents a legacy hardware tier. It offers a stable, dedicated resource profile without burstable limitations, making it qualitatively cost-effective for predictable, low-intensity workloads. This instance is best suited for general-purpose applications, light web serving, and basic database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 66 GB of local storage, suitable for stable, low-intensity workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v3 is a general-purpose VM with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 66 GB local storage, running on Hyper-V.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose virtual machine offering balanced memory and compute resources for stable, legacy enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 32 GB of system memory and a 66 GB temporary storage disk
Supports Premium Storage for high-performance external disk configurations
Delivers average multi-core CPU and small-model LLM inference performance
Best suited for general-purpose applications, light web serving, and basic databases
